subject: fix(pwa): fix post-deployment broken app state
body:
After every Vercel deployment, the PWA got stuck showing no header, no map,
no data, and an offline banner. The only recovery was clearing app data.

Root causes:
- sw.js was a static file that never changed between deployments, so the
  browser never detected a new SW and never showed an update banner
- staleWhileRevalidate for HTML served cached old HTML referencing new JS
  bundle hashes not yet in cache
- SW returned empty Response('', {status: 503}) for failed JS fetches,
  causing silent partial rendering (components fail without visible errors)
- No self-recovery mechanism — with no header visible, users were stuck

Fixes:
- SW: Build-time version injection (inject-sw-version.mjs) so browser
  detects new SW after each deployment
- SW: Auto skipWaiting() on install — broken UI can't show "Update" banner
- SW: Network-first for HTML navigation — always fresh HTML when online
- SW: Versioned cache names — old caches cleaned on activation
- SW: Removed silent 503 fallback — let JS errors propagate naturally
- SW: Proper offline fallback page for navigation requests
- usePWAUpdate: controllerchange listener auto-reloads when new SW activates
- +html.tsx: Inline recovery script forces immediate SW update check +
  fallback cache clear after 15s if app fails to mount
- _layout.tsx: Sets __appHealthy flag for recovery script coordination

Tags: pwa, service-worker, deployment, offline, recovery
Complexity: 4
Urgency: 4
Impact: user-facing
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: pwa
Semver: patch

title: fix post-deployment broken app state
